entries.title: Hoth Base Is Hidden but Vulnerable

context.label: Episode V: The Empire Strikes Back

content.fact: At the opening of Empire, Echo Base is a hidden Rebel base on Hoth but remains vulnerable to Imperial discovery.
content.injection: During early ESB, use Hoth as a temporary, exposed Rebel refuge rather than a stable capital or safe headquarters.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Episode V: The Empire Strikes Back.
Respect story-position bounds from Episode V: The Empire Strikes Back to Episode V: The Empire Strikes Back.
antiLore: Do not treat later-era, alternate-continuity, or fanon facts as true in this Context unless the user explicitly selected that branch.
content.notes: Generated development draft; verify during Saga review.

entries.title: Imperial Probe Leads to Hoth Assault

content.fact: The Empire locates Echo Base through probe intelligence, forcing a Rebel evacuation.
content.injection: After the probe confirms Hoth, the Rebels must evacuate under attack; do not keep Echo Base as a long-term active refuge.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Rebels Evacuate Hoth.
Respect story-position bounds from Rebels Evacuate Hoth to Rebels Evacuate Hoth.
antiLore: Do not treat later-era, alternate-continuity, or fanon facts as true in this Context unless the user explicitly selected that branch.
content.notes: Generated development draft; verify during Saga review.

content.fact: After Hoth, Rebel forces are scattered and pressured rather than operating from a secure base.

content.fact: Luke trains with Yoda on Dagobah, but his training is brief, incomplete, and emotionally disrupted.
content.injection: During and after Dagobah training, Luke improves but remains unfinished; do not write him as a calm, fully trained Jedi Master.

constraints: Training is interrupted before completion.
antiLore: Do not portray ESB Luke as fully trained.

content.injection: Do not have ordinary Rebels, Imperials, or civilians know Yoda’s location unless the story establishes exceptional knowledge.

content.injection: Use the Dagobah cave as psychological/Force symbolism about fear, anger, and Vader-like potential, not as simple tactical prophecy.

entries.title: Han and Leia Romance Develops Under Pressure

context.label: Episode V: The Empire Strikes Back

content.fact: Han and Leia’s romance becomes explicit under Imperial pursuit and Bespin captivity.
content.injection: During ESB and after, Han and Leia may carry romantic tension/commitment, but this is developed under danger and unresolved captivity.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Episode V: The Empire Strikes Back.

entries.title: Cloud City Falls Under Imperial Control

content.fact: During the Bespin crisis, Cloud City falls under direct Imperial pressure and cannot remain politically neutral.
content.injection: After Vader’s arrival at Bespin, Cloud City is unsafe for Rebel-linked characters and subject to Imperial coercion.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Bespin Betrayal.

entries.title: Vader Uses Friends as Trap for Luke

content.fact: Vader uses Luke’s friends at Bespin to lure Luke into confrontation.
content.injection: In ESB Bespin context, frame Han, Leia, Chewie, and the droids as leverage in Vader’s trap for Luke. Do not import later-era status, relationships, factions, or outcomes into this window.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Bespin Betrayal.
Respect story-position bounds from Bespin Betrayal to Bespin Betrayal.
antiLore: Do not let general model memory override the selected Legends story position.
content.notes: Generated development draft; verify during Saga review.

entries.title: Han Frozen and Sent Toward Jabba

content.fact: Han is frozen in carbonite at Bespin and taken by Boba Fett toward Jabba.
content.injection: After Bespin and before the Jabba rescue, Han is unavailable as an active free agent and is a captive bounty object.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Han Frozen in Carbonite.
Respect story-position bounds from Han Frozen in Carbonite to Han Frozen in Carbonite.

entries.title: Luke Loses His Hand on Bespin

content.fact: Luke loses his hand in the duel with Vader and receives a prosthetic afterward.
content.injection: After the Bespin duel, Luke has a cybernetic hand; before this injury, do not describe him with that prosthetic.

entries.title: After Empire, Luke Knows Vader’s Claim

context.label: After The Empire Strikes Back, before Return of the Jedi

content.fact: After Empire, Luke is aware of Vader’s paternity claim but has not yet fully processed Obi-Wan and Yoda’s withheld truth.
content.injection: In the after-ESB/before-ROTJ window, Luke may be troubled by Vader’s claim; do not reset him to ANH ignorance.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: After The Empire Strikes Back, before Return of the Jedi.

context.label: After The Empire Strikes Back, before Return of the Jedi

content.injection: In after-ESB/before-ROTJ scenes, keep Leia’s sibling identity hidden from Leia, Luke, and ordinary characters unless the story reveals it early.
